behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets segmentation logic itself (single parsing boundary), not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified by behavior text focusing only on segmentation outcome, with no persistence, subprocess, or inter-hook exchange.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmenter with a multi-line shell snippet (`VAR=1\nOTHER=2\ngh pr create ...`) and assert it returns discrete statement segments in order rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is segmenter -> trigger-classifier interaction (incident was whole-block `not_a_trigger`). Unit tests on classifier alone can miss bad handoff. REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/network needed; command text can be injected. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is about recognizing command content, not real `gh` execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser+trigger-detection pipeline on a multi-line block containing assignments then `gh pr create`; assert classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the same cross-module boundary as behavior 2 but for negative discrimination (`pr` vs `issue`) after segmentation. Unit-only checks can miss misclassification caused by statement extraction/normalization interplay. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because no real CLI/API behavior is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser+classifier a multi-line assignment block ending in `gh issue create`; assert result is not PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Requires wiring detection + identifier reconstruction + state persistence modules. Local filesystem write/read is sufficient; no real network/subprocess required by behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior says reconstruct/persist identifier for state, not that identifier must come from real `gh` process output.
behaviors.test_description: Given a PR-create trigger event fixture, execute hook path end-to-end in-process and assert persisted state contains expected PR identifier fields required by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is an inter-module contract test (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer) via shared local state. MOCK-HIDE: testing only writer or only reader in isolation can miss schema/field mismatches. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem suffices; no OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond normal file I/O. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text requires readable/enforceable state format, not real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run PostToolUse to write gate state from PR-create input, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement against that state and assert enforcement activates for the same PR context.
